Details: Removal of radium from water by ion exchange can be very effective (62 percent to greater than 99 percent removal). Ion exchange is a Best Available Technology for the control of radium. It is dependent upon the resin type, regeneration frequency, competing ions, and initial concentration. Radium is preferred over calcium, magnesium and other cations by most ion exchange resins. The most common resin used was a cationic, polystyrene-based zeolite resin which performed equally well in the calcium, sodium, and hydrogen form.  Another IEX media commonly used was manganese greensand which was equally effective (84 percent to 98 percent removel).  Although the literature focused on Ra-226, Ra-228 is removed equally as well as both are divalent. Although virgin resin performed well, the best removal was reported by regenerating the resin using sodium chloride or dilute acid.
